answer:
\boxed{Emily Dickinson}

The poem in question is **"Because I could not stop for Death"** (Frag. 712), where Dickinson uses vivid metaphors—including the "catafalqued king" (a funeral bier) and the "solitary man of war" (Death)—to personify Death as a "torpedo" navigating the "waters of death." The imagery reflects her signature themes of mortality and the inescapable march of time.
